summary: Frontend-only testing of AnchurPOS Next.js app. All critical flows tested: login page, manager/crew login, bottom navigation, crew attendance page, public order form, and logout.

minor.issue: Returns 500 - likely missing Firestore composite index for query: where('isRead', '==', false) + orderBy('createdAt'). Shows up in browser console after manager login.

action_items: Fix /api/alerts Firestore query - add composite index for 'isRead' + 'createdAt' in Firestore console, or rewrite query to not require composite index
critical_code_review_comments: Alerts API uses compound Firestore query (where + orderBy on different fields) which requires a composite index. Without the index, it throws 500.
context_for_next_testing_agent: All login flows work. Manager redirects to /manager/dashboard with pink hero card and 4-tab bottom nav. Crew redirects to /crew/attendance with big pink Absen Masuk button and 4-tab bottom nav. Public /order page accessible without login with green header. Logout from crew page works. Only issue: /api/alerts returns 500 due to missing Firestore composite index.
rca_of_issue: GET /api/alerts?unread=true fails with 500. Root cause: Firestore compound query uses .where('isRead','==',false).orderBy('createdAt','desc') which requires a composite index that hasn't been created. Fix: add composite index in Firebase console (Collection: alerts, Fields: isRead ASC + createdAt DESC) or simplify query to only orderBy without the where clause and filter in memory.
